title: acer negundo subsp. californicum
intro: Acer negundo subsp. californicum, also known as California boxelder, is a subspecies of boxelder (Acer negundo) native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of California boxelder include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies like teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
